title: NOC 33101 – Medical Laboratory Assistants and Related Technical Occupations | GTR Immigration
description: Medical Laboratory Assistants and Related Technical Occupations under NOC 33101 are essential healthcare support professionals responsible for specimen collection, laboratory preparation, and routine diagnostic testing across Canada. With expanding diagnostic services and increased laboratory demand, this TEER 3 occupation continues to offer stable employment opportunities nationwide. For internationally trained candidates, NOC 33101 may qualify under Express Entry and selected Provincial Nominee Programs, providing a pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER3 – Occupations usually require college certificate or on-the-job trainingMajor Group33 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesSub-Major Group331 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesMinor Group3310 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesNOC Code33101

duties: Medical Laboratory Assistants perform some or all of the following duties:
Collect, label, and prepare blood, tissue, and other biological samples
Conduct routine laboratory tests and analyses
employment_requirements: Completion of a college certificate program in medical laboratory science
Certification by the Canadian Society for Medical Laboratory Science is usually required by employers
Internationally educated applicants may need credential assessment and employer-recognized certification prior to employment.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $38,500 to $77,000, depending on province, work setting, and experience.
job_outlook: Growth in research and public health initiatives
Certified professionals with phlebotomy or specialized laboratory experience may experience stronger employment opportunities.
labour_market_demand: Medical Laboratory Assistants are employed in:
This positive employment gap indicates steady demand, particularly in provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, Quebec, and Newfoundland and Labrador.
Why Immigrate to Canada as a Medical Laboratory Assistant?
Strong demand in public and private healthcare sectors
Opportunities in hospitals and research institutions
As diagnostic services expand across Canada, laboratory support staff remain in consistent demand.
pathways: If you are qualified under NOC 33101, you may be eligible for immigration pathways to Canada through Express Entry or Provincial Nominee Programs.
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your pathway toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 33101.
